page: Microsoft Azure F4s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FSv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL processor cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the CPU includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1D cache. The hardware configuration is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 33 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core and memory subsystem capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU test scored 1409.49, and the Geekbench single-core score reached 1144.0, both placing in the average tier. However, multi-core performance is limited, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 3661.48 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 2517.0, both categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, registering 25.4 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 20.93 GB/sec for large block write operations. Cryptographic and database operations also fall into the weaker performance tiers, as evidenced by a Passmark CPU encryption score of 1710.41 and a Redis SET throughput of 3.49 million operations per second.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the F4s_v2 is structured for compute-bound workloads that do not demand massive memory footprints. It is suitable for light web serving, single-threaded batch processing, and basic software compilation. However, memory-intensive databases, large-scale multi-threaded applications, and modern LLM inference tasks will face performance bottlenecks due to the low memory capacity and weak multi-core scaling. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a dedicated compute entry point without the overhead of unused RAM, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable CPU access on a constrained budget.
description: Microsoft Azure F4s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the FSv2 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It provides 4 vCPUs (2 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory and 33 GB of local storage. The instance supports Premium Storage but does not include integrated GPUs. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1144.0, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results, such as a multi-core Geekbench score of 2517.0 and uncached read speeds of 25.4 GB/sec. This hardware profile is best suited for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements, such as light web serving, single-threaded batch processing, and basic utility scripting, offering a dedicated CPU allocation without the cost of excess memory.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F4s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 33 GB of local storage, designed for entry-level compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F4s_v2 is a compute-optimized VM with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 33 GB of local storage.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized virtual machine featuring a balanced entry-level CPU profile and low memory overhead for targeted workloads.
bullet_points: Features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 33 GB of local storage.
Delivers average single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core scaling.
Premium Storage capable with no bundled GPU or public IPv4 addresses.
Best suited for light web serving, single-threaded batch jobs, and basic compilation.
